title: Example 13-8 bevel gear bearing reaction analysis on the gear shaft

pitch_angle_geometry.notes: Use these radii only to compute bevel pitch angles gamma and Gamma.

transmitted_load_inputs.notes: Use this radius in the pitch-line velocity equation V = 2*pi*r*n/12 for the textbook Example 13-8 load calculation.

unknown_moments.name: T_y
unknown_moments.note: Shaft torque balance about the shaft axis

notes: Upgraded to separate pitch-angle geometry from transmitted-load geometry.
The previous file incorrectly used average_pitch_radius_pinion_in = 3.0 in for pitch-line velocity. That produced the wrong tangential force.
For Example 13-8, the textbook load calculation uses r = 1.293 in in V = 2*pi*r*n/12.
Support C is the thrust bearing on the analyzed gear shaft, so it is the only support allowed to react the y component.
